title: PathItem
description: Describes the operations available on a single path.

__schema1.title: Reference
__schema1.description: A reference to another component in the specification.

__schema10.title: Operation
__schema10.description: Describes a single API operation on a path.

__schema11.title: tags
__schema11.description: A list of tags for API documentation grouping.

__schema13.title: summary
__schema13.description: A short summary of the operation. Keep under ~120 characters.

__schema14.title: description
__schema14.description: A verbose description. CommonMark syntax MAY be used.

__schema15.title: operationId
__schema15.description: Unique identifier for the operation. Used for code generation.

__schema16.title: parameters
__schema16.description: Parameters for this operation, combined with path-level parameters.

__schema18.title: Reference Object
__schema18.description: A reference to another component in the specification.

examples.description: A user is a type of person.
A pet is a type of animal.
A pet is a type of animal.

__schema19.title: summary
__schema19.description: A short summary which by default SHOULD override that of the referenced component.

__schema20.title: description
__schema20.description: A description which by default SHOULD override that of the referenced component.

__schema23.title: name
__schema23.description: The name of the query parameter. Case-sensitive.

__schema25.title: description
__schema25.description: A description of the parameter. CommonMark syntax MAY be used.

__schema30.description: Allows empty values (?param=). Deprecated in favor of schema-level validation.
__schema30.title: allowEmptyValue

__schema33.title: explode
__schema33.description: For arrays/objects. When true, each value gets its own parameter.

__schema34.description: When true, allows reserved characters without percent-encoding.
__schema34.title: allowReserved

__schema37.title: Schema Object
__schema37.description: A Schema Object defines the shape of a JSON value.

__schema38.title: String Object
__schema38.description: A String Object defines the shape of a string value.

__schema39.title: title
__schema39.description: A title for the schema, used for documentation.

__schema40.title: description
__schema40.description: A description of the schema. CommonMark syntax MAY be used.

__schema41.title: default
__schema41.description: The default value for this schema.

__schema42.title: example
__schema42.description: An example value for this schema.

__schema43.title: enum
__schema43.description: An array of valid values for this schema.

__schema46.title: Discriminator
__schema46.description: When request bodies or response payloads may be one of a number of different schemas, a discriminator object can be used to aid in serialization, deserialization, and validation.

__schema47.title: propertyName
__schema47.description: The name of the property in the payload that will hold the discriminator value.

__schema48.title: mapping
__schema48.description: An object to hold mappings between payload values and schema names or references.

__schema53.title: XML
__schema53.description: A metadata object that allows for more fine-tuned XML model definitions.

__schema54.title: name
__schema54.description: Replaces the name of the element/attribute used for the described schema property.

__schema55.title: namespace
__schema55.description: The URI of the namespace definition.

__schema56.title: prefix
__schema56.description: The prefix to be used for the name.

__schema57.description: Declares whether the property definition translates to an attribute instead of an element.
__schema57.title: attribute

__schema59.description: May be used only for an array definition. Signifies whether the array is wrapped or not.
__schema59.title: wrapped

__schema63.title: ExternalDocumentation
__schema63.description: Allows referencing an external resource for extended documentation.

__schema64.title: description
__schema64.description: A short description of the target documentation.

__schema65.title: url
__schema65.description: The URL for the target documentation.

__schema67.title: readOnly
__schema67.description: When true, the property is only returned in responses, not accepted in requests.

__schema68.title: writeOnly
__schema68.description: When true, the property is only accepted in requests, not returned in responses.

__schema69.title: deprecated
__schema69.description: When true, indicates this schema is deprecated and should be avoided.

__schema70.title: nullable
__schema70.description: OpenAPI 3.0: Set to true to allow null values.

__schema72.title: format
__schema72.description: Semantic format hint for string values. Common formats: date-time, email, uuid, uri, hostname, ipv4, byte, binary.

__schema73.title: Pattern
__schema73.description: A regular expression pattern the string must match.

__schema79.title: Number Object
__schema79.description: A Number Object defines the shape of a number value.

__schema81.title: format
__schema81.description: Number precision format. Use 'float' for 32-bit single-precision or 'double' (default) for 64-bit double-precision IEEE 754.

__schema82.title: multipleOf
__schema82.description: Require the number to be a multiple of this value.

__schema84.title: minimum
__schema84.description: Inclusive lower bound for the number.

__schema86.title: maximum
__schema86.description: Inclusive upper bound for the number.

__schema88.title: exclusiveMinimum
__schema88.description: When true, the minimum value is exclusive (value must be > minimum).

__schema90.title: exclusiveMaximum
__schema90.description: When true, the maximum value is exclusive (value must be < maximum).

__schema92.title: Integer Object
__schema92.description: An Integer Object defines the shape of an integer value.

__schema94.title: format
__schema94.description: Integer size format. Use 'int32' for signed 32-bit (-2B to 2B) or 'int64' for signed 64-bit integers.

__schema95.title: multipleOf
__schema95.description: Require the integer to be a multiple of this value.

__schema97.title: minimum
__schema97.description: Inclusive lower bound for the integer.

__schema99.title: maximum
__schema99.description: Inclusive upper bound for the integer.

__schema101.title: exclusiveMinimum
__schema101.description: When true, the minimum value is exclusive (value must be > minimum).

__schema103.title: exclusiveMaximum
__schema103.description: When true, the maximum value is exclusive (value must be < maximum).

__schema105.title: Boolean Object
__schema105.description: A Boolean Object defines the shape of a boolean value.

__schema107.title: Array Object
__schema107.description: An Array Object defines the shape of an array value.

__schema117.title: Object Object
__schema117.description: An Object Object defines the shape of an object value.

__schema129.title: Composition Schema
__schema129.description: A schema using composition keywords (allOf, anyOf, oneOf, not) without a type constraint.

__schema130.title: allOf
__schema130.description: Require the instance to validate against all subschemas.

__schema132.title: anyOf
__schema132.description: Require the instance to validate against at least one subschema.

__schema134.title: oneOf
__schema134.description: Require the instance to validate against exactly one subschema.

__schema136.title: not
__schema136.description: Require the instance to NOT validate against the given subschema.

__schema139.title: examples
__schema139.description: Multiple named examples. Mutually exclusive with 'example'.

__schema146.title: externalValue
__schema146.description: A URL that points to the literal example.

__schema148.title: content
__schema148.description: For complex parameters. Mutually exclusive with 'schema'.

__schema150.title: MediaType
__schema150.description: Each Media Type Object provides schema and examples for the media type identified by its key.

__schema158.title: Encoding
__schema158.description: A single encoding definition applied to a single schema property.

__schema159.title: contentType
__schema159.description: The Content-Type for encoding a specific property.

__schema162.title: Header
__schema162.description: The Header Object follows the structure of the Parameter Object.

__schema163.title: Header
__schema163.description: The Header Object follows the structure of the Parameter Object.

__schema164.title: description
__schema164.description: A brief description of the parameter.

__schema165.description: Determines whether this parameter is mandatory.
__schema165.title: required

__schema167.description: Specifies that a parameter is deprecated.
__schema167.title: deprecated

__schema169.description: Sets the ability to pass empty-valued parameters.
__schema169.title: allowEmptyValue

__schema171.title: style
__schema171.description: Describes how the parameter value will be serialized.

__schema198.title: name
__schema198.description: The name of the path parameter. Must match a template expression in the path.

__schema200.title: description
__schema200.description: A description of the parameter. CommonMark syntax MAY be used.

__schema201.description: Path parameters must always be required.
__schema201.title: required

__schema205.title: explode
__schema205.description: For arrays/objects. When true, each value gets its own parameter.

__schema208.title: examples
__schema208.description: Multiple named examples. Mutually exclusive with 'example'.

__schema210.title: content
__schema210.description: For complex parameters. Mutually exclusive with 'schema'.

__schema214.title: name
__schema214.description: The name of the header. Case-insensitive.

__schema216.title: description
__schema216.description: A description of the parameter. CommonMark syntax MAY be used.

__schema221.title: style
__schema221.description: Serialization style. Only 'simple' is valid for headers.

__schema222.title: explode
__schema222.description: For arrays/objects. When true, each value gets its own parameter.

__schema225.title: examples
__schema225.description: Multiple named examples. Mutually exclusive with 'example'.

__schema227.title: content
__schema227.description: For complex parameters. Mutually exclusive with 'schema'.

__schema233.title: description
__schema233.description: A description of the parameter. CommonMark syntax MAY be used.

__schema238.title: style
__schema238.description: Serialization style. Only 'form' is valid for cookies.

__schema239.title: explode
__schema239.description: For arrays/objects. When true, each value gets its own parameter.

__schema242.title: examples
__schema242.description: Multiple named examples. Mutually exclusive with 'example'.

__schema244.title: content
__schema244.description: For complex parameters. Mutually exclusive with 'schema'.

__schema257.title: responses
__schema257.description: REQUIRED. The responses for this operation.

__schema259.title: Response
__schema259.description: Describes a single response from an API Operation.

__schema260.title: Response
__schema260.description: Describes a single response from an API Operation.

__schema268.title: Link
__schema268.description: The Link object represents a possible design-time link for a response.

__schema269.title: Link
__schema269.description: The Link object represents a possible design-time link for a response.

__schema270.title: operationId
__schema270.description: The name of an existing, resolvable OAS operation, as defined with a unique operationId.

__schema271.title: operationRef
__schema271.description: A relative or absolute URI reference to an OAS operation.

__schema272.title: parameters
__schema272.description: A map representing parameters to pass to an operation as specified with operationId or identified via operationRef.

__schema275.title: requestBody
__schema275.description: A literal value or {expression} to use as a request body when calling the target operation.

__schema279.title: url
__schema279.description: A URL to the target host. Supports server variables in {braces}. May be relative.

__schema280.title: description
__schema280.description: An optional description of the server.

__schema281.title: variables
__schema281.description: A map of server variables for URL template substitution.

__schema283.title: ServerVariable
__schema283.description: A server variable for URL template substitution. Use {variableName} in the server URL.

__schema284.title: enum
__schema284.description: An enumeration of valid string values for this variable.

__schema286.title: default
__schema286.description: The default value for substitution if not supplied.

__schema287.title: description
__schema287.description: An optional description for the server variable.

__schema294.title: Callback
__schema294.description: A map of possible out-of-band callbacks related to the parent operation.

__schema295.title: Callback
__schema295.description: A map of possible out-of-band callbacks related to the parent operation.

__schema297.title: deprecated
__schema297.description: Marks this operation as deprecated. Defaults to false.

__schema299.title: security
__schema299.description: Security requirements for this operation. Overrides root-level security.

items.title: SecurityRequirement
items.description: Lists the required security schemes for this operation.
